questions.question: Cosine similarity between two vectors measures:
options: The angle between vectors -- high similarity if they point in the same direction
questions.explanation: cos(theta) = A.B / (||A||*||B||). Normalised dot product. Range -1 to 1. 1 = identical direction, 0 = orthogonal, -1 = opposite.

questions.question: Sentence embeddings differ from word2vec by:
options: Encoding full sentence meaning as a single vector, not individual words
questions.explanation: Word2Vec: one vector per word token. Sentence transformers: one vector per sentence/paragraph, capturing full semantic meaning.

questions.question: The embedding dimension (e.g. 384, 1536) represents:
options: The length of the dense vector representing each text
questions.explanation: Higher dimensions = richer representation but more memory and slower search. all-MiniLM-L6: 384 dims. text-embedding-3-large: 3072 dims.

questions.question: ChromaDB stores data:
options: By default in local files (SQLite + Parquet) -- portable, no server needed
questions.explanation: ChromaDB is an embedded vector DB -- default local mode needs no server. Persistent with chromadb.PersistentClient(path='./db').

questions.question: Approximate Nearest Neighbor (ANN) search is used in vector DBs because:
options: Exact nearest-neighbor over millions of vectors is too slow -- ANN trades tiny accuracy loss for massive speedup
Embeddings are not precise enough for exact search
questions.explanation: HNSW, IVFFlat, LSH are ANN algorithms. 99%+ recall at 100x+ speedup over brute-force exact search.

questions.question: Hybrid search combines:
options: Dense vector search (semantic) + sparse keyword search (BM25) -- best of both
questions.explanation: Keyword search finds exact terms. Vector search finds similar meaning. Hybrid catches both cases -- important for specific named entities.

questions.question: Metadata filtering in vector search:
options: Restricts search to documents matching metadata conditions (e.g. source='textbook', date>'2024')
questions.explanation: Filter: retrieve from only 'course=ml' chunks. Combines semantic similarity with structured filters -- like SQL WHERE for vector search.

questions.question: text-embedding-3-small vs text-embedding-3-large (OpenAI):
options: small is faster/cheaper (1536d); large is more accurate but 2x more expensive (3072d)
questions.explanation: Match model to task. For RAG over millions of chunks, small is often sufficient. For ranking tasks requiring precision, large wins.

questions.question: Embedding models should be chosen based on:
options: Benchmark scores on your specific task type (MTEB leaderboard) + language support + cost
questions.explanation: MTEB leaderboard ranks embeddings across retrieval, classification, clustering tasks. Match to your actual task type.

questions.question: Supabase pgvector enables:
options: Storing embeddings in PostgreSQL with vector similarity search -- if already on Supabase, no extra DB needed
questions.explanation: pgvector adds vector type and similarity search operators to Postgres. Perfect for apps already using Supabase -- unified DB.
